steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The blank separates 'style' (noun) from 'terse' (adjective) followed by a list. On the left, we have a noun phrase; on the right, a participial phrase acting as an adjective.

steps.title: Apply the colon rule
steps.content: A colon introduces an example or elaboration of what precedes it. Choice D uses a colon correctly to introduce the three elements defining Brooks's poetic style. The other choices violate this rule: a comma (A) separates list items, a period (B) breaks the sentence unnaturally, and a semicolon (C) joins independent clauses—none fit the need to introduce an example.
